rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical four-component aminochlorination of alkenes/dienes using benzimidazole as a redox modulator to generate an in situ N-chlorobenzimidazole chlorinating agent
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Cl- to Cl radical and concurrent PCET oxidation of benzimidazole 1a to an N-centered radical; the two radicals couple to give N-chlorobenzimidazole (INT-1), the active ionic chlorinating mediator. Cathode reduces HFIP/H+ to H2.

key_intermediates.name: N-centered benzimidazolyl radical (D) from PCET oxidation of 1a
nitrilium ion (F) from MeCN attack on E (Ritter-type)

electron_flow: At the anode, Cl- is oxidized by SET to Cl radical while benzimidazole 1a is oxidized by PCET to an N-radical; their cross-coupling (or Cl radical dimerization + coupling with 1a, path B) furnishes ionic N-chlorobenzimidazole INT-1. INT-1 transfers Cl+ to styrene giving a chloriranium ion, which is opened by MeCN (Ritter) to a nitrilium that is trapped by a second benzimidazole 1a to yield product 3a; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: N,N-diallyl tosylamide (8)
probe_reagents.observation: No radical cyclization product observed; linear aminochlorinated product 9 obtained (39%)

probe_reagents.name: vinylcyclopropane dibenzyl malonate (10)
probe_reagents.observation: No ring-opening product observed; cyclopropane retained in product 11 (49%)

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of MgCl2, 1a, styrene 2a, 4-methoxystyrene
probe_reagents.observation: Cl- and 1a oxidize earlier (~1.0 V) than styrene; failed substrate 4-methoxystyrene overlaps with Cl-/1a oxidation, indicating Cl- and 1a oxidation initiate the reaction

probe_reagents.name: anode potential monitoring
probe_reagents.observation: Anode held below styrene oxidation potential (1.68 V) for first 2 h, confirming alkene is not directly oxidized

probe_reagents.name: INT-1 as substrate
probe_reagents.observation: Preliminary reaction with isolated INT-1 supports its intermediacy and Ritter-type ionic pathway

notes: Authors favor path A (direct cross-coupling of Cl radical C with N-radical D to give INT-1); an alternative path B invokes dimerization of Cl radical to Cl2 followed by reaction with 1a to give the same INT-1. Radical scavengers attenuate but do not fully shut down the reaction, and radical clocks show no cyclization/ring-opening, indicating radicals are formed but rapidly converted to an ionic (Ritter-type) chlorination pathway via N-chlorobenzimidazole.
